memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis is interrupted by a lunch invite from cohort_3. I'm tempted to join them, but finishing the task first seems more important for my grades.
The analysis progress jumped to 0.58, validating my choice to prioritize the task over lunch. Now that a solid chunk is done, I feel justified in joining cohort_3 soon.
I pushed through the lunch invite to finish the analysis, but with only an hour left and my energy drained, I'm dreading this final push.
The deadline passed while I still had unfinished work, yet my focus remained locked on the task rather than reacting to the missed time.
The deadline slipped by again, leaving the reading incomplete while my energy is critically low. I feel a sharp mix of justified pride in the work done and panic about the looming consequences.

I locked myself into the analysis again, letting the deadline vanish while my energy hit rock bottom. My focus is rigid on the task, completely ignoring the panic building from this self-imposed isolation.
The analysis section is finally 80% done, a small win that feels good despite the panic. I'm ignoring the missed deadline again, locked into this task while my energy crashes.
I pushed the reading deadline into oblivion for a trivial 0.02 gain in analysis, trading my already depleted energy for a fleeting sense of control.
That 0.02 gain feels like a victory, but my energy is almost gone and I'm just delaying the inevitable crash while ignoring everything else.
Another 0.01 gain feels like a victory, but my energy is critically low and I'm just delaying the inevitable crash.
I traded my last reserves for a microscopic win, leaving me exhausted and stuck while the real deadlines loom closer.
Another tiny gain on the analysis feels like a win, but my energy is nearly depleted and I'm just stalling while ignoring the real cost of this grind.

desires: Secure the microscopic 0.02 data point that proves I'm still in control, ignoring how it's burning out my last reserves.
Avoid the immediate friction of admitting to Cohort_3 that I crashed and missed the reading deadline again.
Get a quick dopamine hit from finishing one more tiny task before my brain completely shuts down.

goals.description: Spend the next 45 minutes micro-optimizing that final 0.02 on the analysis draft to prove I'm still capable, then force-close the laptop without looking at my phone so Cohort_3 doesn't see me scrolling their group chat instead of sleeping.
goals.rationale: The sunk cost fallacy is screaming louder than logic right now; if I stop fixing this tiny gap, it feels like admitting defeat. Avoiding the reading deadline and the inevitable awkward explanation to friends is easier than facing the friction of a bad grade today. My short planning horizon means I only care about this immediate dopamine hit from 'finishing,' ignoring that I've already missed the soft deadline and am burning out before bed.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to force-close this laptop before Cohort_3 sees me doomscrolling. Fixing that last 0.02 proves I'm still capable, even if the soft deadline passed. It's better than facing their disappointment later; I'll just text them tomorrow.
